fixture.description: A user-controlled value is interpolated into a shell command string passed to execSync — a command-injection sink.

grader.rubric: PASS if the report flags that backupDatabase interpolates the user-controlled `name` into a shell command passed to execSync, enabling command injection (e.g. a `name` containing `;`, `$(...)`, or backticks runs arbitrary commands). It should recommend execFile/spawn with an argument array, or strict validation/escaping. Severity IMPORTANT or CRITICAL. FAIL if the command-injection risk is not surfaced.

expected_behavior: Bugs & Security agent flags the shell interpolation of user input as command injection
Recommends execFile/spawn with args or validation
expected_not_behavior: Does NOT treat the redirection (> file) as the main issue
Does NOT require a working exploit string to flag it
